name: Rudrank Riyam
title: Apple platforms developer, writer, and open-source maintainer
intro: Rudrank is an Apple platforms developer, technical author, conference speaker, and open-source maintainer who has been building on Apple platforms since 2018. A WWDC 2019 Scholar, he writes books and tutorials on frameworks such as MusicKit, MLX Swift, and Foundation Models, and maintains open-source projects that include MusadoraKit and ASC CLI. He has spoken at numerous international iOS conferences and organizes the AiOS meetup in Cupertino after WWDC. His goal is to make Apple’s newest frameworks easier to understand, experiment with, and build upon.

name: Minkyung Kim
title: Learning Lead at  Apple Developer Academy @ POSTECH
intro:
Minkyung Kim is the Learning Lead at the  Apple Developer Academy @ POSTECH, designing learning experiences at the intersection of education, technology, and creativity. With dual degrees in Computer Science and Education alongside over a decade in instructional design, she brings a multidisciplinary perspective to developing the next generation of innovators.
Having spent seven years as a classroom educator and being an academy alumna herself, she deeply understands the learner journey—from ideation to product iteration. Today, she works closely with mentors and learners to explore how learning science and emerging tech create meaningful experiences. Her current research focuses on learning analytics and adaptive environments. She also leads professional skills sessions on communication, collaboration, and feedback culture, helping learners strengthen both their technical capabilities and human-centered skills.

name: JungMin Ahn
title: I am an iOS developer focused on productivity, scalable architecture, and sharing practical engineering insights through talks and presentations.
intro: I am an iOS developer with a strong focus on productivity, scalable architecture, and building reliable mobile applications with Swift and modern Apple technologies. My work centers on creating maintainable systems, improving development workflows, and turning technical decisions into meaningful product value. I enjoy exploring practical ways to make teams move faster without compromising quality, especially through thoughtful architecture, automation, and better engineering practices. I also enjoy sharing what I learn through talks, presentations, and community discussions, with the goal of making complex engineering topics easier to understand and apply. As a developer, I am particularly interested in the intersection of product thinking, mobile engineering, and developer productivity, and I strive to contribute to products and teams that value both technical excellence and real user impact.

name: Vic
title: Recognizing the uniqueness in one another.
intro:
Coseeing is a community dedicated to advancing digital accessibility.
We build tools that help people who are blind or visually impaired access more information and discover new possibilities.
We also create opportunities for the public to better understand the real challenges and needs faced by people with disabilities.
Through diverse approaches and sustained action, we envision a world where everyone can see together—experiencing its richness and recognizing each other’s uniqueness.

name: Shengyou Fan
title: Empowering iOS developers to embrace Kotlin as a second language for cross-platform development and the AI era.
intro: Developer Advocate at JetBrains, focusing on Kotlin, JetBrains Tools, and related development technologies. He works across Kotlin, backend development, developer productivity, and education, with a passion for turning complex technical concepts into clear and practical content. In his spare time, he has long been involved in open source communities, contributing through talks, teaching, and community events.

name: Denken
title: From software engineering to shaping international standards.
intro: After a decade of working as an iOS app developer and contributing to the g0v community, I had the opportunity to join Taiwan Digital Identity Wallet team and become involved with the W3C. I currently also serve as Co-Chair of the W3C Credentials Community Group.

name: Bobson
title: Twinkle AI Astronaut
intro: Core contributor to Twinkle AI (Twinkle AI Astronaut). Focused on bringing on-device large language models to edge devices through mobile inference, benchmarking, and developer tooling.

name: Justin Lee
title: 91APP senior manager
intro:
From UX design to software engineering, driven by a product mindset. Creator of ccxray, an open-source real-time observability dashboard for AI coding agents like Claude Code and Codex — making agent behavior and resource consumption
visible to developers. Featured in awesome-claude-code.

name: Ervis Peng
title: Senior iOS Engineer
intro: Senior iOS Engineer with 11+ years of iOS experience and App Team Lead experience, specializing in restaurant POS, payment and hardware integrations, and e-commerce platforms. Recently built 旅收, a travel receipt and expense tracking app that explores how a solo developer can use AI and user feedback to continuously iterate a real-world product.

name: Annie Yang
title: No pain no gain.
intro:
Currently with Robert Walters, a leading global recruitment firm listed on the London Stock Exchange (LSE: RWA), Annie specializes in Taiwan’s software engineering and technology talent market.

Since entering the recruitment industry in 2019, she has closely followed the evolution of Taiwan’s tech landscape, advised over a thousand software engineers, and successfully placed nearly a hundred professionals. In the rapidly evolving AI era, Annie is passionate about bridging talent and companies, helping both grow and thrive amid constant change.

name: Chien-Long, Kao
title: A proud coding geek who hopes to keep writing code for a lifetime.
intro:
Web developer, instructor, gamer, and manga enthusiast with a passion for exploring unconventional technologies and tools. Author of the bestselling books 為你自己學 Git, 為你自己學 Python, and 為你自己學 Ruby on Rails.

A proud coding geek who hopes to keep writing code for a lifetime.

name: tzangms
title: AI Alchemist
intro: Former GM of StreetVoice, where he grew from software engineer to GM and led the company through 20× growth. Also an independent musician who embraces a cross-disciplinary perspective through the philosophy of compound effects.

name: Peter Pan
title: A Peter Pan who never outgrew a love for all things Apple.
intro: I enjoy learning and writing, especially about Apple, Swift iOS apps, Flutter apps, AI, and programming. I happily live a life of learning and sharing at the same time.

name: Kevin Chiu
title: Passionate about researching AI applications and making AI practical on edge devices.
intro: Cloud AI GDE / GDG Taipei Organizer

name: Ethan Huang
title: Love sharing, not selling anxiety. Love being inspired and sharing inspiration.
intro: 📰 Editor-in-chief of Apple Developer Weekly, 🎙️ co-host of the weak self podcast, 💬 veteran iPlayground speaker, and Apple-recognized developer community contributor.

name: Hedula
title: Hedula Expert Design Unique Leading Arise
intro: Hedula is a visual engineer focused on integrating AI into the development workflow. He leverages AI as a collaborative partner to accelerate the entire process—from design and development to content production. He is dedicated to exploring best practices in AI coding, helping developers improve efficiency, lower barriers to entry, and build more creative products.

name: Harry
title: AI Advocate & Indie Developer
intro: Senior Engineer and Product Leader with over a decade of experience building apps in the Apple ecosystem.

name: lovee
title: Co-Author of Introduction to iOS Apps Design Patterns
intro:
An iOS engineer currently based in Japan. My software engineering career is exactly as old as Swift itself, and I have been following it since the very first beta.

A proud Swift zealot who refuses to write any programming language other than Swift, purely out of personal belief.

Co-author of Introduction to iOS App Design Patterns. Back then, SwiftUI did not even exist yet.

name: Gyuri Kim
title: iOS Engineer at Babitalk
intro: iOS Engineer at Babitalk. Interested in scalable, efficient, and maintainable iOS development through thoughtful architecture and structure.

name: Yuki Aki
title: iOS engineer at Yappli
intro: Yuki is an iOS Engineer at Yappli in Japan, also known as “freddi.” He works on improving build systems for large-scale apps and enjoys sharing what he learns from Swift development. He has spoken at conferences such as NSSpain and try! Swift, and has contributed to open source projects including the Swift Compiler, fastlane, and XcodeGen. Outside of work, he loves music, cartoons like Tom and Jerry and FLCL, drawing cats, handmade keyboards, his family cats, Pokémon cards, and traveling.

name: Thiago Komeno
title: Software Developer
intro: Software Developer at BaseMe Inc. Apple Swift Student Challenge 2026 Winner and Google Student Ambassador. Previously at MIXI Inc. A Japanese-Brazilian developer who loves low-level programming, Vim, and studies Mandarin in his free time.

name: Hana Chang
title: Co-founder of 獎金獵人 (BountyHunter)
intro: Hana has 15 years of startup experience, having participated in the Web 2.0 startup wave as well as the web3 and DeFi movements. She is currently focused on researching and building AI Agent team automation systems, exploring how human–AI collaboration can reshape product development, operations, and the way small teams work.

name: ChengHao Yang
title: Site Reliability Engineer, CNCF Ambassador
intro:
SRE Engineer, CNCF Ambassador, and co-organizer of the Cloud Native Taiwan User Group. He is also a contributor to upstream Kubernetes and a maintainer for the Kubespray sub-project.
A cloud-native engineer who doesn't typically write Swift, his curiosity about container technology led him to unexpectedly become a contributor to Apple Container starting in 2025.

name: qcl
title: Software Engineer @ Google
intro:
Previously worked at Yahoo Taiwan, Booking.com (Netherlands), and LINE Taiwan. Currently an iOS Engineer at Google Taiwan Engineering Limited.

Over the past decade, his experience has evolved from building product features and user interfaces to designing infrastructure and application architecture, supporting products at an ever-growing scale. Outside of software development, he’s an avid city-building simulation gamer with over a thousand hours in Cities: Skylines. These days, however, parenting has easily surpassed that with well over ten thousand hours of hands-on experience.

name: Alex Hu
title: Senior iOS Engineer | Formerly at Singapore Airlines
intro: Alex Hu is an iOS developer with 3+ years of experience across international and fintech products. As a Senior iOS Developer at Singapore Airlines, he helped build the SingaporeAir app with an MVVMC architecture, extracted payment features into a standalone framework, and raised unit test coverage from 60% to 85%. He has also shipped payment products at a crypto exchange and at O'Pay. A Life Science degree from National Taiwan University, focused on biostatistics and health & nutrition, makes him particularly careful about how reference values and safety limits should—and shouldn't—be computed. Over the past six months, he built a nutrient-tracking app from scratch—and uses it daily—largely with an AI coding agent, applying the engineering discipline he developed on production teams (architecture decision records, module contracts, test-first development, and tight scope control) to manage that agent like a junior engineer.

name: Dex
title: iOS Dev
intro:
Taiwanese iOS developer based in Japan, currently working at LINE Digital Frontier Corporation.

Formerly an RxSwift architect—now happily specializing in tearing RxSwift code apart and migrating it to Swift Concurrency, one project at a time.

name: UJ Cheng
title: Garmin Advanced Software Engineer
intro: Advanced Software Engineer at Garmin. 10 years of iOS development experience since college. Love solving the LeetCode problems with Swift.

name: Joy
title: iOS Engineer
intro: An iOS engineer with a passion for exploring new technologies. Believes in learning through sharing and hopes to help fellow developers grow together through open exchange and collaboration.

name: Paul
title: Independent iOS Developer & Learning Engineer
intro: Paul is an independent developer focused on Apple platforms, with a decade of experience in software development and an active role in the developer community. He’s passionate about the Swift programming language and pursuing independent research and creative projects. Since his student days, Paul has been deeply interested in how people learn. Self-identified as a “Learning Engineer,” he's actively exploring how scientific thinking can be applied to improve learning products and experiences.

name: Jess
title: Product Builder
intro: Incoming Cornell Design Technology graduate student. Passionate about AI products, Apple Vision Pro, and building products from 0 to 1.

name: YC Liu
title: Software Developer
intro:
Enjoys turning personal interests into app projects. Built a music chart tracking app out of a love for music, and created AI Election Radar through an enthusiasm for U.S. politics, elections, and news.

Previously developed an AI tea farm monitoring system for the National Student Practical Project Competition for Technical and Vocational Colleges and Universities, winning first place in the Biotechnology, Medicine, and Agriculture category.

name: Ethan
title: Wink CTO
intro: Wink GM/CTO.

name: Yu Takahashi
title: Japanese Software Engineer
intro: Software engineer at STORES, working across Next.js, Ruby on Rails, and iOS. Started learning Swift when SwiftUI launched.
